concerns.text: T016 computes checksums only for 'raw dataset files under data/' but Constitution Principle III requires checksums for ALL files under data/ (including processed and output). This silently relaxes the data hygiene constraint.

T069 creates real-world validation set but does not include the required stratification across five domains (tech, e-commerce, finance, healthcare, SaaS) specified in FR-031b and SC-031b. This is a semantic weakening of the validation requirement.

T044 implements bias-adjustment but does not explicitly include the subsampling action required by FR-027 when a domain exceeds 30%. The spec mandates 'either subsample that domain or flag a violation' - this action is missing from the task.

T048 creates Quickstart guide but does not explicitly capture the 'novice user verification' requirement in SC-028. Measuring time alone does not verify novice capability - the task must include verification by a novice user.

Task ID T026 is duplicated with conflicting purposes (Phase 2: Resource-Monitoring Module vs Phase 3: Synthetic Validation Dataset Generation). Task ID T062 is also duplicated (Phase 2: Monte Carlo validation module vs Phase 3: Contract test for manifest schema). This creates implementation ambiguity.

T069 creates data/manual_validation/real_world_labels.csv while T072 references data/manual_validation/annotated_summaries.csv for the same validation set. File path inconsistency violates single-source-of-traceability.

T099 validates external citations but does not confirm the Reference-Validator Agent runs at all three Constitution-specified points (artifact write, Advancement-Evaluator gate, research_review→research_accepted transition). This silently relaxes the Verified Accuracy gate.

T027 specifies 'exactly 10,000' synthetic samples while FR-030 requires 'at least 10,000'. This is a constraint tightening (acceptable) but should be documented as intentional rather than appearing as a spec deviation.
